    create an integer variant containing a variable and a constant
    void
    testVariant_05()
    variants must not refer to disappearing variables
    void
    testVariant_06()
    convergent events that refine convergent events do not require variants (bug# 1946656)
    void
    testVariant_07()
    if there is no convergent event, then there need not be a variant (bug# 1946656)
    void
    testVariant_08()
    if there is no convergent event, then there need not be a variant
    void
    testVariant_09_bug2689872()
    create a variant containing an illegal character
    void
    testVariant_10()
    Create an variant with an empty expression.
    void
    testVariant_11()
    A variant is not needed even when the INITIALISATION is marked as convergent (which is faulty and fixed by the static checker).
    void
    testVariant_12()
    There is no risk of conflict between a variant label and a variable with the same name.
    void
    testVariant_13()
    There can be a conflict between the default variant label and an invariant label with the same label.
    void
    testVariant_14()
    There can be a conflict between a variant label and an invariant with the same label.
    void
    testVariant_15()
    There can be a conflict between the default label of a variant and an event with the same label.
    void
    testVariant_16()
    There can be a conflict between a variant label and an event with the same label.
    void
    testVariant_17()
    There can be a conflict between the default label of two variants.
    void
    testVariant_18()
    There can be a conflict between two variants having the same label.
    void
    testVariant_19()
    One can have two variants in the same machine.
    void
    testVariant_20()
    If one of two variants is incorrect, it is ignored, but the valid variant is kept.
    void
    testVariant_21()
    If both variants are incorrect, they are removed and a warning is issued to indicate that a variant is missing.
    void
    testVariant_22()
    With two valid variants and no convergent event, both variants are marked as not necessary.

    • testVariant_11

      public void testVariant_11() throws Exception
      A variant is not needed even when the INITIALISATION is marked as convergent (which is faulty and fixed by the static checker).
      Throws:
      Exception

    • testVariant_12

      public void testVariant_12() throws Exception
      There is no risk of conflict between a variant label and a variable with the same name.
      Throws:
      Exception

    • testVariant_13

      public void testVariant_13() throws Exception
      There can be a conflict between the default variant label and an invariant label with the same label. This is a regression introduced in version 3.4. The variant is then erased by the static checker.
      Throws:
      Exception

    • testVariant_14

      public void testVariant_14() throws Exception
      There can be a conflict between a variant label and an invariant with the same label.
      Throws:
      Exception

    • variantInvariantLabelConflict

      private void variantInvariantLabelConflict(String label) throws org.eclipse.core.runtime.CoreException
      Throws:
      org.eclipse.core.runtime.CoreException

    • testVariant_15

      public void testVariant_15() throws Exception
      There can be a conflict between the default label of a variant and an event with the same label. This is a regression introduced in version 3.4. The event is then erased by the static checker.
      Throws:
      Exception

    • testVariant_16

      public void testVariant_16() throws Exception
      There can be a conflict between a variant label and an event with the same label.
      Throws:
      Exception

      public void testVariant_21() throws Exception
      If both variants are incorrect, they are removed and a warning is issued to indicate that a variant is missing.
      Throws:
      Exception

      public void testVariant_22() throws Exception
      With two valid variants and no convergent event, both variants are marked as not necessary.

      Exception
